title: Cassine albens
intro: Cassine albens, also known as White Cassine or Cassine, is a small, deciduous shrub native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Cassine albens include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cassine albens is often prepared as a decoction or infusion in traditional herbal medicine, and it can also be made into a syrup or tincture for easier consumption.
taxonomy: Cassine albens is classified as a plant in the family Anacardiaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the cashew family. This family consists of around 70 genera and 830 species. Cassine albens is a species within the genus Cassine, which is a group of flowering plants native to Africa and southern parts of the world.
